message:
fix(nats,rate-limit): harden NATS protocol parsing and fix zero-period rate limiter stall

NATS client security hardening:
- Add `validate_nats_token()` to reject subjects, reply-to addresses,
  and queue groups containing whitespace or control characters, which
  prevents NATS protocol injection (e.g., a subject containing \r\n
  followed by a PUB command could inject arbitrary messages)
- Validate subject/reply-to/queue-group on publish, publish_with_reply,
  request, subscribe, and queue_subscribe paths
- Reject MSG headers with more fields than the protocol allows (subject,
  sid, [reply-to], bytes) to catch malformed server responses early
- Validate MSG payload terminator is exactly \r\n to detect truncated
  or corrupted frames rather than silently accepting garbage
- Reject empty host in NatsConfig::from_url() to fail fast on
  malformed connection URLs like "nats://:4222"
- Add tests for all new validation paths including the CRLF injection
  vector, empty strings, and whitespace/control character rejection

Rate limiter: when `period_nanos == 0` (meaning "no throttling"),
ensure at least 1 token is available even when `rate == 0`, so
`poll_ready` never stalls on a degenerate zero-rate, zero-period
configuration. Previously `self.tokens = self.rate` would set tokens
to 0, causing the service to block forever.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
